faqs.question: How can I get admission to the college?
faqs.answer: Admissions are based on merit in qualifying exams (12th grade or entrance test scores). Apply online through our official website by filling the admission form, uploading documents, and paying the application fee.

faqs.question: What is the eligibility criteria for admission?
faqs.answer: For undergraduate courses, candidates must have passed 10+2 with at least 60% marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics. For postgraduate courses, a relevant bachelor's degree with a minimum of 55% marks is required.

faqs.question: What is the step-by-step application process?
faqs.answer: 1) Visit the college website and click 'Apply Now'. 2) Register with your email and mobile number. 3) Fill personal and academic details. 4) Upload documents. 5) Pay the application fee. 6) Download the confirmation slip.

faqs.question: What is the last date to apply for admission?
faqs.answer: The admission window usually opens in May and closes in the last week of July. Exact dates are published on the college website each academic year.

faqs.question: What documents are required for admission?
faqs.answer: You will need your 10th and 12th mark sheets, transfer certificate, migration certificate, category certificate (if applicable), passport size photographs, and Aadhar card copy.

faqs.question: Is there an entrance exam for admission?
faqs.answer: Yes, admission to engineering programs requires a valid score in a recognized entrance exam such as JEE Main or the state-level CET. MBA admission requires CAT/MAT/CMAT scores.

faqs.question: Is lateral entry admission available?
faqs.answer: Yes, diploma holders and B.Sc graduates can apply for lateral entry directly into the second year of the relevant B.Tech program, subject to seat availability.

faqs.question: What courses are offered by the college?
faqs.answer: We offer B.Tech in Computer Science, Information Technology, Electronics & Communication, Mechanical, and Civil Engineering, along with M.Tech, MBA, and MCA programs.

faqs.question: What departments does the college have?
faqs.answer: The college has Computer Science & Engineering, Information Technology, Electronics & Communication, Mechanical Engineering, Civil Engineering, Management Studies, and Computer Applications departments.

faqs.question: How long is the B.Tech course?
faqs.answer: The B.Tech program is a 4-year full-time course divided into 8 semesters. The M.Tech and MBA programs are 2-year courses divided into 4 semesters.

faqs.question: How many seats are available in each course?
faqs.answer: Each B.Tech branch has an intake of 60 to 120 students per year, depending on the department. MBA and MCA programs have an intake of 60 students each.

faqs.question: Where can I find the course syllabus?
faqs.answer: The detailed syllabus and curriculum for every course is published under the 'Academics' section of the college website and is also available from your department office.

faqs.question: Are online or part-time courses available?
faqs.answer: Currently, all our programs are full-time, on-campus courses. We do not offer online or distance-education programs at this time.

faqs.question: What is the fee structure?
faqs.answer: The annual tuition fee ranges from ₹80,000 to ₹1,50,000 depending on the course and category. Hostel and transport fees are charged separately.

faqs.question: How can I pay the college fees?
faqs.answer: Fees can be paid online through the student portal using net banking, debit/credit card, or UPI. Offline payment via demand draft is also accepted at the accounts office.

faqs.question: Can I pay the fee in installments?
faqs.answer: Yes, the fee can be paid in two installments per academic year: one at the beginning of the odd semester and one at the beginning of the even semester.

faqs.question: Is there a fee refund policy?
faqs.answer: Yes, if admission is cancelled before the start of classes, the fee is refunded as per UGC/AICTE guidelines after deducting a processing charge.

faqs.question: Is there a late fee for delayed payment?
faqs.answer: A late fee penalty of ₹500 per week is applicable if fees are not paid by the due date mentioned in the academic calendar.

faqs.question: Are scholarships available?
faqs.answer: Yes, we offer merit-based scholarships (up to 50% fee waiver), need-based financial aid, and government scholarships for eligible categories.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for a scholarship?
faqs.answer: Students scoring above 90% in qualifying exams, or those from economically weaker sections with valid income certificates, are eligible to apply for scholarships.

faqs.question: How do I apply for a scholarship?
faqs.answer: Scholarship applications open at the start of every academic year on the student portal. Submit the form along with supporting documents before the deadline.

faqs.question: How are the placement opportunities at the college?
faqs.answer: Our Training & Placement Cell has an average placement rate of 90%. Over 150 companies including TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Amazon, and Cognizant visit our campus every year.

faqs.question: What is the highest package offered to students?
faqs.answer: The highest package offered in the most recent placement season was ₹42 LPA. The average package stands at around ₹6 LPA.

faqs.question: What is the eligibility for placements?
faqs.answer: Students must have a minimum of 60% aggregate marks with no active backlogs to be eligible to sit for campus placement drives.

faqs.question: Which companies visit the campus for recruitment?
faqs.answer: Companies such as TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Cognizant, Amazon, Accenture, Capgemini, and several core engineering firms regularly visit our campus.

faqs.question: Does the college provide internship opportunities?
faqs.answer: Yes, internships are mandatory in the pre-final and final years. The Placement Cell helps students secure internships with reputed companies for hands-on industry experience.

faqs.question: What training programs does the college offer?
faqs.answer: The college conducts technical and soft-skills training, aptitude sessions, mock interviews, and certification workshops in collaboration with industry experts.

faqs.question: What hostel facilities are available?
faqs.answer: We have separate hostels for boys and girls with 24/7 security, Wi-Fi, hygienic mess facilities, RO drinking water, and recreation rooms. Single, double, and triple sharing rooms are available.

faqs.question: What is the hostel fee?
faqs.answer: Hostel fees range from ₹60,000 to ₹90,000 per year depending on room sharing type, and include accommodation and mess charges.

faqs.question: What are the hostel rules and curfew timings?
faqs.answer: The hostel gate closes at 9:30 PM on weekdays and 10:30 PM on weekends. Students must sign the entry/exit register and carry their ID card at all times.

faqs.question: Is transport facility available?
faqs.answer: Yes, the college operates buses covering major routes across the city with fixed pickup and drop points. Transport fees vary depending on distance from the college.

faqs.question: How can I find my bus route and timing?
faqs.answer: Bus route maps and pickup timings are available at the transport office and are also shared with students at the start of each academic year.

faqs.question: What are the library facilities?
faqs.answer: Our central library has over 50,000 books, national and international journals, a digital e-library, a quiet reading hall, and a reference section. It is open from 8 AM to 8 PM.

faqs.question: How many books can a student borrow at a time?
faqs.answer: Students can borrow up to 4 books at a time for a period of 15 days using their library card, with a renewal option if no one else has requested the book.

faqs.question: How qualified is the faculty?
faqs.answer: Our faculty comprises highly experienced professors, many with Ph.D. qualifications and years of industry or research experience, supported by regular faculty development programs.

faqs.question: What is the student-to-faculty ratio?
faqs.answer: The college maintains a healthy student-to-faculty ratio of approximately 15:1 to ensure personalized attention for every student.

faqs.question: What are the college campus timings?
faqs.answer: The college operates Monday to Saturday, 9:00 AM to 9:00 PM. The administrative office follows the same hours and is closed on Sundays and public holidays.

faqs.question: What is the daily class schedule?
faqs.answer: Classes are held in periods of 55 minutes each, starting at 9:15 AM, with a lunch break from 12:45 PM to 1:30 PM, and the last period ending at 4:15 PM.

faqs.question: How are examinations conducted?
faqs.answer: Examinations are conducted twice a semester: internal assessments (mid-semester) and final semester exams, following the university academic calendar.

faqs.question: When are exam results declared?
faqs.answer: Results are usually declared within 4-6 weeks after the exams and are published on the student portal and notice board.

faqs.question: Can I apply for revaluation of my answer sheet?
faqs.answer: Yes, students can apply for revaluation within 10 days of result declaration by submitting a form and the prescribed fee at the examination cell.

faqs.question: What if I fail a subject or have a backlog?
faqs.answer: Students with a backlog can appear for the supplementary/re-exam conducted after the main semester exams, as per the academic calendar.

faqs.question: What is the minimum attendance requirement?
faqs.answer: Students must maintain a minimum of 75% attendance in each subject to be eligible to appear for the semester examinations.

faqs.question: What happens if my attendance is below 75%?
faqs.answer: Students with attendance between 65-75% may apply for condonation with a valid medical or personal reason, subject to approval by the department and principal.

faqs.question: What are the college holidays?
faqs.answer: The college follows the official academic calendar with holidays for major national and regional festivals, a winter break in December-January, and a summer break from May to June.

faqs.question: How can I contact the college?
faqs.answer: You can reach us at Chairman: +91-98495-55252, Office: +91-96037-66552, Email: hq.principal@gmail.com, or visit the office during working hours (Mon - Sat, 9 AM - 9 PM).

faqs.question: Where is the college located?
faqs.answer: Avanthi's Research and Technological Academy is located at Basavapalem Village, Bhogapuram Mandal, Vizianagaram, Andhra Pradesh 531162.

faqs.question: Is there a canteen on campus?
faqs.answer: Yes, our campus canteen serves hygienic and affordable food including breakfast, lunch, snacks, and beverages, along with a separate food court offering multiple cuisines.

faqs.question: What sports facilities are available?
faqs.answer: The college has a sports complex including a cricket ground, football field, basketball and volleyball courts, indoor games room, and a fully-equipped gymnasium.

faqs.question: What clubs and activities can students join?
faqs.answer: Students can join the Coding Club, Robotics Club, Music & Dance Club, Drama Club, Photography Club, and the Entrepreneurship Cell, which organize regular events and competitions.

faqs.question: Is Wi-Fi available on campus?
faqs.answer: Yes, high-speed Wi-Fi is available across the entire campus including classrooms, library, hostels, and common areas.

faqs.question: What laboratory facilities does the college have?
faqs.answer: Each department has well-equipped laboratories with modern equipment, including computer labs, electronics labs, mechanical workshops, and research labs.

faqs.question: Is there a medical facility on campus?
faqs.answer: Yes, the college has an on-campus medical room with a resident nurse and first-aid facilities, with a tie-up with a nearby hospital for emergencies.

faqs.question: How do I get my student ID card?
faqs.answer: Student ID cards are issued during orientation week after admission is confirmed. Lost ID cards can be reissued at the administrative office for a small fee.

faqs.question: How do I get a Transfer/Bonafide Certificate?
faqs.answer: Transfer Certificates and Bonafide Certificates can be requested through the student portal or the administrative office and are usually issued within 3-5 working days.

faqs.question: How can I raise a complaint or grievance?
faqs.answer: Students can submit complaints through the Student Grievance Cell form available on the college website, or approach the department HOD directly for quick resolution.

faqs.question: Does the college have an alumni network?
faqs.answer: Yes, the college has an active Alumni Association that organizes annual meets, mentorship programs, and networking events connecting current students with graduates.

faqs.question: Greeting
faqs.answer: Hello! Welcome to the College Enquiry Chatbot. I can help you with admissions, courses, fees, placements, hostel facilities, and much more. What would you like to know?

faqs.question: Thanks
faqs.answer: You're most welcome! Feel free to ask if you have any more questions about the college.
